doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: This sentence has to describe unequivocally a condition that the system should meet

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: Whenever possible, every requirement should define a unique condition

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: In the same way, the sensor and actuator requirements define the objectives that must be achieved by the sensing and actuation subsystems respectively

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: Finally, the validation requirements specify the conditions under which the performance of the system will be tested

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: Global requirements R101: The system shall work in strip-cropping systems

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R102: The system shall operate on selected winter (for example, leeks, beans and cabbage) and summer crops (i.e., tomato, beans and pumpkin)

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: The system shall move along crop rows with widths in the range of 1-1.4 meters, heights between 0.2 and 0.6 meters and lengths between 10 and 100 meters

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: The system shall operate without damaging the plants

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: The system shall collect information of the spatial and time heterogeneities in the crops. R106: The system shall recognize crops and weeds. R107: The system shall determine the type, location and health of the plants

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R108: The system shall early detect plant problems. R109

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: The system shall provide information about the health of the crops, such as some symptoms of diseases: e.g., water stress and abnormal colors

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R110: The system shall apply liquid treatments to the crops

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R111: The treatments will be applied to the ground around the target plant

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R112: The system shall consist of a wheeled cart, a manipulator robot and the required components for sensing and actuation tasks

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R113: The system shall have a protection of IP54 against solid particles and liquids

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: Cart requirements R201: The cart shall consist of a structure of bars with four wheels, similar to the gantry systems used in controlled traffic farming or pivot irrigation systems

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: The cart shall have an axle track in the range of 1.2 to 1.5 meters to move along the rows without damaging the crops. R203

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: The cart shall have clear height of 1 to 1.4 meters over the ground to hold the robot, sensors and actuators that must work over the plants

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R204: The cart shall have a wheelbase of at least 1 meter to be able to collect data of the plants in a circle with a diameter of 1 meter. R205: The cart shall carry a payload of at least 120 kilograms, which includes the estimated weights of the manipulator (25 kg), the components required to power and control it (25 kg), the tank for the liquid treatments (60 kg), the actuators required to apply them (5 kg) and the sensors and cameras (5 kg)

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: Robot requirements R301: The robot shall hang down from the structure of the cart. R302: The robot shall reach the plants to acquire data and apply treatments

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R303: The robot shall be able to place its tool within a circle with a diameter of 1 meter at a height of 0.2 meters over the ground

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R304: The robot shall have a reach (i.e., a distance between the base and the tool) of at least 600 millimeters

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R305: The robot shall have a precision of at least 10 millimeters in the positioning of its tool

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: The robot shall have a maximum weight of 50 kilograms, including not only the electromechanical components, but also the power and control equipment

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R307: The robot shall have a load capacity of 1.5 kilograms in operation positions to carry out sensing and actuation elements

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R308: The robot shall be able to reach most of the points inside the workspace defined by previous requirements with different orientations of the tool

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R309: The robot shall be protected from dust and water jets. ## 3.4

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: Sensor requirements R401: As a proof of concept, the prototype shall focus on only one sensing technology

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: The prototype shall integrate a camera to take multispectral images

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R403: The prototype shall be able to take pictures of the crops in a circle with a diameter of 1 meter and below the cart with enough resolution to distinguish separate plants

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: Actuation requirements R501: As a proof of concept, the prototype shall focus on only one plant treatment

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R502: The prototype shall be able to spray a liquid treatment

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: The prototype shall actuate on the ground around the plants

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: Processing and decision-making requirements R601: The operator shall be able to supervise the operations of the system remotely. R602: The operator shall be able to start/stop the complete system at any moment

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R603: The operator shall dictate the targets where specific measurements and treatments are required to the robot

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R604: The robot shall plan the movements to reach the goals autonomously

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R605: The sensing system shall work continuously and autonomously during the operation

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: The operator shall be able to start/stop the actuation system

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R607: The system shall identify each plant and provide an index regarding its state, as well as recommend a treatment

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: Communication and interface requirements R701: The interface shall show the relative location of the cart in the strip cropping field: e.g., provide the number of the crop row and the distance from the beginning

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R702: The interface shall show real-time relative position of the robot to the cart, including both the states of its joints and the position and orientation of its tool. R703

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: The interface shall show all the camera images and sensors measurements. R704

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: The interface shall allow the operator to send pose goals to the tool of the robot

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R705: The interface shall allow the operator to activate/deactivate the treatment spray

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: The interface shall allow the operator to stop the system in case of emergency

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R707: All the systems shall be communicating using a Wi-Fi network

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R708: The software shall be developed by using common assets in the world of robot planning and control

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R709: The software shall be developed in modules, so it can be expanded to add new functions

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: The system shall be validated through both component and global tests

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R802: The tests shall take place in a strip-cropping field in Madrid (Spain)

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R803: The requirements of the robot shall be validated independently in laboratory experiments and integrated in the system in field experiments

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R804: The robot shall be validated by dictating 20 randomly generated pose goals within its workspace and measuring its precision (defined as the distance between commanded and reached poses) (R303, R304 and R305)

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R805: The sensing system shall be validated in field experiments

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R806: The requirements of the sensing system (R402, R403 and R404) shall be validated by mapping a complete crop row as defined by R102 and R103 R807

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: The actuation system shall be validated in field experiments

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: R808: The requirements of the actuation system (R502 and R503) shall be validated spraying 10 plants and determining its precision (defined from the difference between the real and target areas of the treatment)

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: In order to meet the requirements (R101-R113), the system will be made up of a cart, a manipulator robot, a tank with spraying system, sensors and cameras

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: It must be highlighted that this is a functional design, so the locations, shapes and sizes of the different elements can vary in the prototype. !

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: [img-1.jpeg](img-1.jpeg) Figure 1: Functional design of the robotic prototype The cart will be made up of a metal frame (bars) and four wheels (R201-R204)

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: The frame will support the different elements (R205) and will not rely on an autonomous traction system (R206)

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: As shown in Figure 1, the robot will hang down from the structure (R301) and will be able to reach the ground to acquire data and apply treatments (R302)

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: In addition, a minimum protection against solid particles and liquids of IP54 is chosen, which means the robot is effective against both dust (ingress of dust is not entirely prevented, but the quantity remains low enough to not interfere with the satisfactory operation of the equipment) and water splashing (water splashing against the enclosure from any direction shall have no harmful effect)

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: Finally, the robot must be able to perform trajectories from point to point, as well as to follow straight lines and splines

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: According to the preliminary design (Figure 4) and the specifications of the manufacturer (Igus), the robot will have a reach of 790 millimeters (R304) and a precision of 1 millimeter (R305)

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: Additionally, it will weight around 20 kg (R306), including the electromechanical components and excluding the control and power elements, and have a load capacity of around 2.5 kg (R307). !

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: [img-6.jpeg](img-6.jpeg) Regarding the sensors, a multispectral camera will be used to obtain multispectral images and compute NDVI indices of plants (R401-R402)

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: Additionally, a laser scanner will be integrated in the cart to obtain 3D models of the soil and plants, which will ease the motion planning of the manipulator robot and the application of treatments in the adequate locations (R404)

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: The actuation system will consist of a tank containing the liquid treatment, a hose pipe from the tank to the robot tool, and a pump to spray the liquid (R501 and R502)

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: the manipulator robot will reach the position and orientation where the sprayer should apply the treatment (R503)

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: An operator interface will be developed to monitor and control the system work (R601)

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: This interface will allow the operator to start/stop the system (R602), send pose goals to the robot (R603) and activate/deactivate the actuator (R606)

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: The interface will provide the operator with information about the state of the cart (R701) and robot (R702)

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: Additionally, it will show the 3D model of the crop row and the multispectral images of the plants (R703)

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: A set of algorithms will be developed to plan the trajectories of the robot to the pose goals (R604), process the data collected by laser scanner and multispectral camera (R605) and discover the information about the plants' state and recommended actions (R607)

doc_id: D4.1 Definition of specifications for robotic prototype.pdf
original_text: These algorithms will be developed by using Robot Operating System, which can be considered as a standard in robot programming (R709)
